commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make the new diary-history prompt read use a symlink-safe/non-file-safe path and add the missing regression coverage.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete prompt-data exposure risk by reading Dream Diary history through a helper that follows symlinks before the existing write safety check.; Review finding: [P1] Guard diary reads before adding them to the prompt — exte... (sha=31a75706f4be49b25d3706de9ebd01c10d1a6c90)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core light dreaming to read recent Dream Diary entries, prefer not-yet-diarized short-term recall snippets before the phase limit, pass sweep and diary context into the narrative prompt, and add focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +129, Tests +149. Total +278 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main limits light candidates by recall recency/count before consulting DREAMS.md, and the prompt has no diary-history context. I did not execute the repro because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make the new diary-history prompt read use a symlink-safe/non-file-safe path and add the missing regression coverage.

target.title: fix #83830: [Bug]: Dreaming diary repeats "first day" narrative every sweep — same early memories dominate snippets
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: light dreaming could keep sending the same high-recall first-day memory into the Dream Diary generator after that memory was already written, so repeated sweeps kept producing the same "first day" narrative.
- Solution: make light dreaming read recent Dream Diary entries, expand the candidate pool before the phase limit, prefer snippets not already covered by recent diary history, and pass the current sweep day plus recent diary context into the narrative prompt.
- What changed: `runLightDreaming` now de-emphasizes recently diarized snippets without dropping them as fallback candidates; `buildNarrativePrompt` can include diary continuity context; tests cover the real short-term recall + DREAMS.md path.
- What did NOT change: public memory-core config, plugin SDK API, provider/model routing, DREAMS.md markers/file format, short-term recall store schema, and append/dedupe cleanup commands are unchanged.
- Out of scope: deleting or rewriting existing diary entries, adding semantic duplicate cleanup to append, changing provider/model generation quality, changing recall persistence schema, or changing public dreaming defaults.
- Why it matters / User impact: later memories can reach Dream Diary snippets after repeated sweeps instead of the same early "first meeting" memory monopolizing the one-page input.
- Fixes #83830

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A high-recall first-day memory that was already present in `DREAMS.md` previously won the light phase's limited snippet slot because selection sorted by recent recall time and recall count before the phase limit. The diary generator then received the same early memory again, so repeated sweeps could replay the same first-day narrative.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw worktree on branch `feat/issue-83830`. The proof executed production TypeScript sources from this worktree with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-83830-evidence/clean-openclaw-config.json`, a temporary real memory workspace under `/tmp`, real `DREAMS.md`, and memory-core SQLite plugin state configured through the same test runtime used by memory-core production-path tests.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

    OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-83830-evidence/clean-openclaw-config.json node --import tsx --input-type=module <local proof script> > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-83830-evidence/local-dream-diary-repeat-proof.clean.log 2>&1

    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts

    pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:src

    pnpm lint --threads=8

    pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts
    git diff --check

- **Evidence after fix:**

    local-dream-diary-repeat-proof.clean.log:
    {
      "oldLimitOneSelection": [
        {
          "snippet": "初次见面时，我第一次醒来并认识了主人。",
          "recallCount": 4
        }
      ],
      "promptChecks": {
        "freshSnippetInMemoryFragments": true,
        "staleFirstDayInMemoryFragments": false,
        "staleFirstDayInRecentDiaryContext": true,
        "hasCurrentSweepContext": true
      },
      "dailyLightBlockChecks": {
        "freshCandidateWritten": true,
        "staleCandidateWritten": false
      },
      "dreamsChecks": {
        "generatedNarrativeAppended": true,
        "existingFirstDayEntryPreservedAsHistory": true
      },
      "loggerMessages": {
        "warn": [],
        "error": []
      }
    }

    targeted-vitest.log:
    Test Files  3 passed (3)
    Tests  177 passed (177)
    [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 45.88s

    typecheck.log:
    $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
    $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo

    lint.log:
    [oxlint:core] finished
    [oxlint:extensions] finished
    [oxlint:scripts] finished

    format-and-diff-check.log:
    All matched files use the correct format.
    git diff --check completed with no whitespace errors.

- **Observed result after fix:** With `light.limit=1`, the old limit-first ranking would have selected the already-written first-day snippet with `recallCount=4`. After this patch, the production light sweep selected the later routing snippet as the memory fragment, kept the first-day text only as recent diary context, wrote the fresh candidate into the light block, and appended a new diary entry while preserving the existing first-day entry as history.
- **What was not tested:** A long-running HTTP gateway listener and an external provider/model prose-quality run were not started. The proof records the prompt sent through the production subagent surface and returns a deterministic assistant response so the evidence can assert selection, prompt context, and `DREAMS.md` append behavior without making the result depend on model wording.

## Main sync conflict verification

- **Conflict addressed:** `origin/main` moved DREAMS.md path/locking helpers into `dreaming-dreams-file.ts`; this update keeps `readRecentDreamDiaryEntries()` on that source-of-truth by reading through `updateDreamsFile(... shouldWrite:false)` instead of reintroducing private path/file helpers.
- **Verification command:** `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91225 exec vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts`
- **Observed result:** `Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 99 passed (99)`.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** ClawSweeper's issue review asked for focused memory-core repair plus live `DREAMS.md`/store proof. This PR adds the production-path local proof above and regression coverage for the first-day dominance path.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** a real short-term recall store contains a high-count first-day snippet and a lower-count newer snippet, `DREAMS.md` already contains the first-day snippet, and a light dreaming sweep with `limit=1` sends the newer snippet to the narrative prompt while keeping the first-day text only as diary history.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** it covers the owner selection boundary, prompt context boundary, and real `DREAMS.md` append path without changing config/schema or relying on provider-specific prose output.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, compatibility, availability.
- **Risk explanation:** session-state applies because this changes which short-term memory snippets are staged into Dream Diary entries. Compatibility risk is low because public config, file markers, store schema, plugin SDK API, provider routing, and append/dedupe commands are unchanged. Availability risk is low because the change reads the existing diary file during a phase that already reads/writes `DREAMS.md`, and covered snippets remain fallback candidates when no fresher candidate is available.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** this PR does not delete existing diary content, does not add semantic duplicate removal to `appendNarrativeEntry()`, does not change external provider/model behavior, does not migrate `DREAMS.md` or short-term recall state, and does not change public dreaming defaults.
- **Why acceptable:** the diff is limited to memory-core dreaming narrative/phase files and targeted tests. It preserves existing recall sorting inside each fresh/covered bucket, only widens the pre-limit candidate pool, and the related open PR scan found no overlapping open PRs for the same public surface (`public-contract-related-prs.json` reported `related_open_prs: []`).

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** the failing chain was in the light dreaming source-of-truth boundary: `runLightDreaming()` sorted live short-term recall entries by `lastRecalledAt` and `recallCount`, sliced to the phase limit, and only then generated the diary prompt. Because Dream Diary had no feedback into that selection step, an early first-day memory that was repeatedly recalled could keep winning the limited input slot even after `DREAMS.md` already contained that same memory. `buildNarrativePrompt()` then gave the subagent snippets/themes/promotions without the current sweep day or recent diary history, so the model had no local context that the first-day framing had already been written.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** the fix moves the freshness decision to the source-of-truth phase selection boundary, before the limited snippet set is written and before the narrative prompt is built. It expands the pre-limit candidate pool, partitions entries by whether recent diary history already covers them, preserves the original recall/time ranking inside each partition, and passes current sweep + recent diary context into the prompt. Already-diarized snippets are de-emphasized, not deleted, so genuine recurrence can still appear when it is the best remaining input.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** No guard/catch/default fallback was added to mask repeated output. The `return []` paths in `readRecentDreamDiaryEntries()` are the canonical empty-history result for `limit=0`, missing diary markers, or no existing diary blocks; they do not suppress a failed generation or hide a duplicate. The `return false` path in `isEntryCoveredByRecentDiary()` means an empty snippet cannot be considered already covered. The patch changes candidate ordering and prompt context at the memory-core dreaming owner boundary, and leaves external provider behavior, append semantics, and manual exact dedupe behavior unchanged.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth owner is `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts` for light snippet selection and `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts` for prompt/DREAMS.md context. Public contract scope is narrow: no SDK/API/config/protocol/schema/default migration changed; out of scope are semantic append dedupe, diary rewrites, provider/model behavior, and store migrations.
target.author: mushuiyu886
